sentences.text: Good afternoon, Paul.
How are you
doing?
Well, how are you?
I'm doing good.
It's a beautiful sunny day here, so I have no complaints on my end.
What about you after your week?
Yeah, it's, uh,
actually a storm just cleared up here, so sun's starting to poke out now.
So I, I guess instantly in a better mood.
There you go.
Where are, where are you based out of?
I'm in Montgomery, Alabama.
If you can't tell by the twang in my voice.
Yeah, that's, uh, a small world.
You sound like half of my family because, uh, my mom was born and raised in Birmingham.
So half my family lives out in, uh, Birmingham, Montgomery, Alabama.
So very, grew up, grew up going there all the time.
Ok.
Where are you right now?
Uh, so I was born and raised in Texas and, um, at the end of last year I moved out to Scottsdale, Arizona.
So, oh, yeah.
Moved a little bit closer to the west coast.
Uh, but still wanted that Texas heat.
So, you know, I had to move to the move to the desert.
Yeah.
Well, cool.
I, uh, I appreciate taking you taking time out of your afternoon to meet with me.
Um purpose of today's call.
I wanna have a back and forth conversation with you around market data, comp data.
Um We spoke to a colleague of mine, Jar.
He kind of filled me in a little bit on y'all's quick uh conversation you had on the phone.
Um So I'm just gonna repeat kind of what I heard.
But when it comes to the collection of this sounds like you guys are doing uh call arounds calling the comps and then manually entering all this data into Excel.
Yeah, for the most part, yeah, calling around and then, yeah, looking at the property website, if they're not as willing to give it the information, then they'll get it from there.
But yeah, manually doing both of those with the property staff.
And let me ask you this, how has that been going as of late?
And the reason I ask is, um, obviously I'm on these calls all day, every day.
And it seems to me from what I'm hearing, the industry as a whole is kind of moving away from call arounds for this data.
So kinda ask your opinion on that.

Yeah, I think for the most part now, I, I would say I haven't asked them specifically this question, but I would imagine that most of them get most of their day just looking at the other properties websites without even calling just because I think they all think the same way that all of our pricing now, most of the properties of, of course, the ones that they know are using pricing software, it changes daily so they can just pull it straight from the website and get that information there.
And then they might call the property only to try to figure out their occupancy.
But that's what I think is happening, but I haven't really asked them specifically.
Yeah.
And uh how uh how frequent is this uh going on or how frequently are the teams supposed to be doing this,
supposed to be doing it like biweekly?
But they probably only do it once a month.
Uh just because we require it for our monthly reporting to send an updated market survey.
But that is also a gray area because I'm pretty sure they just send the same one every once in a while from the previous month.
So it's probably more like once a quarter.
Ok.
Interesting.
And uh
or the reports are sitting out, is that just Excel, I'm assuming?
Yeah, just Excel.
OK.
And uh the that kind of data, the market and comp data.
How are you guys leveraging that?
Are you guys making that for like pricing decisions or walk me through that?
Yeah,
for the most part it's, it's that it's, you know, um generally they only come up when things are going bad
at the property, you know, and then we start figuring out, ok, what are our cops doing?
Is there something going on around us, um, in the market?
And we start drilling into that a little bit more.
Um, so I would say, yeah, whenever a property is doing poorly we'll dive into the market a little bit more in the market survey, figure out what they're charging.
What's a little bit different?
Is everyone experiencing this occupancy slump or concession slump and, yeah, that's pretty much it.
Yeah,
that makes sense.
Um, and then as far as, uh a cent goes as a whole, what, uh, what kind of, what, how many properties do you oversee or manage or walk me through that
we own and manage uh, roughly 30 apartment properties now.
Um, I think it's down to about 28 now.
Um, but, yeah, and I'm involved with all 30 of them.
Ok.
Yeah, cool.
Just wanted to see kind of what, uh, um, kind of how in the weeds you get with, you know, the, the, this kind of data.

Um, is there any sort of conversations or initiative going on internally about looking to move away from the manual calls and, and move to some sort of, uh, software as a service or what's it look like there?
I mean, yeah, we're constantly anything that's manual, you know, we're looking at any type of way to automate just about anything.
So this would kind of fall in that same category.
Really?
Ok.
Yeah.
Um,
but specifically market surveys.
No, I mean, we haven't really, um, specifically brought those up.
Yeah.
Ok.
Fair enough.
I appreciate the transparency.
Yeah.
Yeah.
I always like to tell people, like,

my job is not to sell you software is to get all the information in front of you so you can relay up the chain and if it's a good fit then Borden happy to have additional calls and try to help in any way that I can.
So um that's the reason why I asked that.
Um Let me ask you this.
How familiar are you with apartment IQ?
Uh Not very, not until I guess your colleague reached out and then he sent me a link and I kind of dug into it from there, but that's about it.
OK.
Yeah, fair enough.
I'll uh I'll give you kind of the quick pitch on us what we can do and then I'll share my screen and I'll, I'll walk you through the platform and uh at the end of the call would love to get feedback.
And uh if it makes sense, set up some next steps and if not, if it's better if we depart and we're just friends, I'm totally cool with that too.
How does that sound?
Sounds good.
Great.
So, uh yeah, with the shift in the direction of the market is going with kind of this data being a hot sensitive topic.
Um We've developed a automation market survey tool.
It's been around for about five years.

Uh But essentially with us, what you're getting is a live data feed, not only into your assets, but each specific comp you're tracking for each ultimately to see where each of your units is positioned within each of those specific comp sets.
So there's no need for any sort of manual call arounds data entry, anything like that.
You're getting a live feed of 100% public data that's getting pulled directly from property websites and the tool updates every, every day.
So every 24 hours when you log in, you're looking at the most updated accurate information.
And the last thing is we can essentially track anybody.
So we are tracking about 20 million units every single night.
Um doesn't have to be a customer of ours since it's all public data, as long as a property has a website, we can track it.
So there's no uh you know, apartment IQ network, you get access to this that the other like some other data providers.
Uh we can attract anyone in the States.
So that's a little bit about us.
Uh As far as the data that we can show you, we can give it to you from a property perspective.
Floor plan.
We get as granular as each individual unit and then you touched on this earlier, you mentioned uh concessions, but we track those daily as well.
Ok.
Any kind of questions off the top of your head for me.
Otherwise I will share my screen.
No, I guess the only one right now is, is property website data, the specific property website, the only thing that y'all pull your data from or is it if you can't find it there?
Do you like, try to branch out to apartments.com or something like that to fill in some gaps or is it just strictly the property website?
So
strictly property websites.
However, we do sort of have like a checks and balance in place to where um our company is technically split in two.
So we have a partner IQ that's the data product.
And then we have rentable, which is kind of like seo advertising.
So we have our own I OS through them which we then like double check all the data through that.
So um yeah, so principal has been around for about 10 years apart.
IQ has been around for about five.
Yeah, I think we've maybe used principle in the past but don't I know I'm familiar with it, but
yeah.
Yeah.
Awesome.

Ok.
Yeah,
cool.
So this right here is your apartment IQ landing page.

Um And we offer unlimited seats, unlimited training, we can get you up and running live in about 7 to 10 business days to view any of the data, just click, view everything here will load up.

And then lastly each individual unit, we have a report specifically for that.

Just know that we do have a either a PDF or Excel.

Um Now here are some of the filters that we could utilize within the app.
You can filter any of the data by a specific comp or group of comps or you could filter it down by bedroom bathroom size, rent availability you're built.

And then if you'll notice along the left hand side here, some of these different metrics that we can show you this is user specific, but you can either show or hide the metrics that are most important to you and that won't affect how the rest of your organization sees it.
So if we digest this market survey up top here, these are all of the comps in this specific comp set your subject properties highlighted here in blue.
Along this left hand side, here are some of the different metrics that we can show you things like distance your bill total units rent net effective.

How many of these units are available within the property?
How many are vacant we have pending leases?

Last thing, concession details touched on that, whether it's tied to a unit or a property, we will track all of those as well and doesn't matter if it's a pop up ribbon banner text.

Something else that's worth noting.
I think that you will like with how transparent we are with our data and where we're getting it from.
If you actually click onto any of these comp cards, we'll give you some more information on this comp up top here.
We'll show you some nice averages, things like units, rent square footage price per square foot days on market.
We'll also show you where we're getting all this data from.
So with it being a hot sensitive topic, we wouldn't be 100% transparent.
So you can trace all this data back to the source of where we're pulling it from.
Down below here, we'll show you every single unit that they're currently advertising as available.
So again, it is a great way to be able to log in and you have all this at the touch of your fingertips.
How does this, uh, portion of the market survey compared to what your teams are collecting?
Yeah, it generally has the main points from there.
But, um, and then drills down to the kind of unit specific that I imagine it looks like you kind of have that a little bit lower, um, gonna get into that.

Yeah, but it doesn't, it doesn't get into the, I think they try to get into some of the weeds a little bit but doesn't have as, I guess specific data like the realist of units and some of that, but they try to get as accurate as they can for the least and exposure items.
But for the most part has a little bit more data points.
That's probably a little bit better than ours.
Interesting.

So we will show you how many rate changes have been made.

Are we stuck with the comps that we sign up with?

Your built are renovated total units and then average rent per floor plan.
And so if you'll notice down here, we have the similar properties that you might want to take a look at.

So you can add up to about 30 comps.
